rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SINCLAIR ESQ VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Filure To Comply With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT LINDA CATRON's NMotion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: Denied. Plaintiff violated one discovery order. Evidence of willful non-compliance exists, but is questionable. Terminating sanctions would constitute too harsh a sanction at this time. Plaintiff is urged to comply with further order of the Court. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SINCLAIR ESQ VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Discovery And To Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT LINDA CATRON's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Discovery And To Impose Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: GRANTED. Plaintiff ordered to respond to discovery requests referenced in the motion and pay moving party sanctions of $2,420 within fifteen (15) days. Plaintiff represented at the hearing that he served responses. Defendant denies receiving responses. Plaintiff offered no proof of service. Plaintiff could not state with certainty on what date service of responses occurred. Plaintiff represented service was effected within the last "nine (9) days." Even if true, service would have occurred after defendant filed the instant motion. Thus, sanctions of $2,420 are warranted (5 hours @ $450/hr., plus $170 costs). Because plaintiff produced no evidence of service, an order compelling responses is warranted. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: M DEE SAMUELS VS. RALPH TASHJIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT M SAMUELS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Ms. Samuels has not met her initial burden of proof to show that Mr. Tashjian is barred by the Statute of Limitations under Code of Civil Procedure section 340.6 under the Discovery Rule. Further, there are triable issues of whether Ms. Samuels caused Mr. Tashjian damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTOPHER KING VS. OTIS ELEVATOR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Req. For Production Of Documents And Things To Deft Otis Elevator, (Set Two); Statement And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KRISTOPHER KING's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents And Things To Defendant Otis Elevator, (Set Two). Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Quashng Deposition Supoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, WILLIAM HUTTON, RAYMOND BROWN's Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: It is denied as to all records sought for cases filed from January 1, 2004, through the present. It is granted as to all records sought for cases filed before January 1, 2004. This is without prejudice, one way or the other, to plaintiffs seeking to introduce evidence of any of the cases, regardless of when filed, at trial. It is also without prejudice, one way or the other, to plaintiffs issuing a new subpoena for records for cases filed before January 1, 2004, based on newly discovered evidence. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CANTRELL, HARRIS & ASSOCIATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Verified Petition And Verified Petition For The Inspection And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 3, PETITIONER CANTRELL, HARRIS & ASSOCIATES' Verified Petition For The Inspection And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The petition is denied. The CCP does not permit a party to request documents pre-lawsuit to see if there is a valid lawsuit. The Court also recommends, although it won't put this in an order, that the Girls Chorus voluntarily produce the documents. This might avoid a lawsuit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PACIFIC MERCHANT SHIPPING ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Leave To Intervene In Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 10, INTERVENOR SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS, BRUCE HORTON's motion For Leave To Intervene In Action. Granted. The San Francisco Bar Pilots and Bruce Horton in his capacity as President of the San Francisco Bar Pilots have met the requirements of CCP § 387(a) and (b). The San Francisco Bar Pilots and Bruce Horton in his capacity as President of the San Francisco Bar Pilots have also demonstrated that they have the requisite standing to intervene and are entitled to intervene as a real party in interest. (See Marken v. Santa Monica-Malibu Unified School Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1250, 1267, fn. 13.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RADA KATZ et al VS. ALBERT W CHOW MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANTs ALBERT CHOW, DANIEL BURNHAM COURT SURGERY CENTER's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted in part and denied in part. In light of the ruling on the demurrer to the fourth cause of action, the motion to strike portions of the fourth cause of action is moot. Furthermore, the motion to strike is granted as to portions alleging the negligent infliction of emotional distress of Plaintiff Gladshteyn: (1) Paragraph 41; (2) Paragraph 42, lines 4-5: "¿has also suffered extreme emotional distress due to the potential break-up of his marriage, which was previously a happy one. Following the injuries, he¿"; (3) Paragraph 43, lines 11-12: "¿and he expects to continue to suffer from emotional distress in the future"; and (4) Paragraph 89. The motion to strike as irrelevant is granted as to portions referring to the care of Plaintiffs daughter: (5) Paragraph 45; (6) Paragraph 56; and (7) Paragraph 61. The motion to strike the remaining portions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TRAVELVIVA, LLC VS. ALL CONTINENTS TRAVEL, INC.DBA PICASSO TRAVEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANTs ALL CONTINENTS TRAVEL, INC. DBA PICASSO TRAVEL, RAINER KLEE, HUSEYIN OZYURTCU, EREN AKSOY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the second and third causes of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. Regarding the second cause of action, Plaintiff has properly pled a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing that sounds in contract. (See Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 683-684.) Regarding the third cause of action, promissory estoppel is properly pled as an alternative to the breach of contract cause of action. Regarding the fourth cause of action, without more, a breach of contract cannot be a predicate for UCL liability as a matter of law. (Puentes v. Wells Fargo Home Mortg., Inc. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 638, 645.) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts showing how Defendants' actions were unlawful, unfair or fraudulent in addition to being a breach of contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALLAN MENDIETA VS. NATIONWIDE LEGAL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Litigation Or Alternatively, To Strike Allegations In First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 8, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANTs NATIONWIDE LEGAL, LLC, NATIONWIDE LEGAL, INC.'s motion To Stay Litigation Or Alternatively, To Strike Allegations In First Amended Complaint. Denied as to the motion to stay litigation, and granted with 15 days leave to amend, as to the motion to strike. The instant action and the federal action do not cover the same subject matter as each plaintiff is classified differently as non-exempt and salaried employees respectively. (Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804.) Plaintiff does not oppose the motion to strike and requests leave to amend his allegations. (Opp., p. 7:20 - 8:15). Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is unopposed, and is thus granted. Defendant's Supplemental Request for Judicial Notice was filed with its reply, and is denied as improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
